metadata.name: memeputer_vault
metadata.description: Memeputer creator-fee vault (DBC + DAMM v2 unified claim)

accounts.name: creator
docs: The creator — MUST be FeeLedger.creator_wallet (constraint-enforced
below). This is the spine of D-05 (on-chain user-signed claim).

accounts.name: platform_config
docs: Singleton PlatformConfig — read for claim_fee_bps + platform_fee_recipient.

accounts.name: fee_ledger
docs: FeeLedger PDA for this mint — mutated to record claimed += claimable.
The `creator` Signer is gated against `fee_ledger.creator_wallet` per D-05.

accounts.name: fee_vault
docs: FeeVault PDA — source of the SOL transfers. PDA-signed below via
invoke_signed inside `system_program::transfer` CpiContext.

accounts.name: fee_recipient
docs: Platform fee recipient — must match PlatformConfig.platform_fee_recipient.

accounts.name: recipient
docs: Where the NET proceeds go (per D-07: user controls this — typically
defaults to creator at SDK layer, but creator may direct elsewhere).

accounts.name: mint
docs: The mint this claim is for — used only as a PDA seed.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Operator wallet — pays tx fees.

accounts.name: mint
docs: SPL token mint (PDA seed only).

accounts.name: fee_ledger
docs: FeeLedger PDA — mutated to credit `accrued += sol_claimed` and set
`last_swept_at`.

accounts.name: fee_vault
docs: FeeVault PDA — receives the claimed SOL from Meteora.

accounts.name: meteora_pool
docs: The Meteora pool account — UncheckedAccount because we read raw
bytes (specifically `VirtualPool.is_migrated`) for on-chain
dispatch. Meteora validates this account inside the CPI.

accounts.name: meteora_program
docs: The Meteora program to CPI into — MUST be METEORA_DBC_PROGRAM_ID
(pre-graduation) OR METEORA_DAMM_V2_PROGRAM_ID (post-migration),
matching the dispatch decision below.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Operator (launch wallet) — pays rent for the new accounts.

accounts.name: mint
docs: The token mint this ledger tracks fees for.

accounts.name: fee_ledger
docs: FeeLedger PDA — created here, owned by this program.

accounts.name: fee_vault
docs: FeeVault PDA — manually created via system_program::create_account in
the handler. We use `UncheckedAccount` rather than `SystemAccount`
because Anchor 0.32 forbids `init` on `SystemAccount`.
post-create ownership = system program; payload = zero-byte lamport holder.

errors.name: NothingToClaim
errors.msg: Nothing to claim — claimable balance is zero

errors.name: InsufficientVaultBalance
errors.msg: Insufficient SOL in vault — cannot withdraw below rent-exempt minimum

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Unauthorized — caller is not the configured authority

errors.name: InvalidFeeRecipient
errors.msg: Invalid fee recipient account — must match PlatformConfig.platform_fee_recipient

errors.name: LedgerNotInitialized
errors.msg: Fee ledger has not been initialized for this mint

errors.name: WrongCreator
errors.msg: Wrong creator — signer is not FeeLedger.creator_wallet

errors.name: ClaimBelowMinimum
errors.msg: Claimable amount is below the minimum threshold

errors.name: InvalidPoolAccountData
errors.msg: Meteora pool account data is too small to deserialize

types.name: FeeLedger
docs: Per-mint accrual ledger.
One FeeLedger PDA exists per launched coin (seeds: [b"fee_ledger", mint]).
It records cumulative SOL accrued from Meteora sweeps and cumulative SOL
claimed by the creator. The (accrued - claimed) delta is the creator's
current claimable balance.
Ported (with reductions) from `hey_curve/state/fee_ledger.rs`:
- DROP: `platform_fees_accumulated` / `platform_fees_claimed` (Memeputer
accrues 100% to creator and deducts `claim_fee_bps` at user-claim
time, not at sweep time)
- DROP: `creator_claimed[100]` / `last_creator_claim_at[100]` arrays
(Memeputer is single-recipient per D-03)
- DROP: `swept: u8` (no 90-day expiry sweep — deferred to v2)
- DROP: `trade_count` (analytics, not v1)
- ADD: `creator_wallet: Pubkey` (single recipient; mutable per D-03)
- ADD: `reserved: [u8; 64]` (forward-compat for v2 features)

fields.name: bump
docs: Canonical PDA bump (SEC-01 — stored so claim/sweep ix don't recompute).

fields.name: mint
docs: The SPL token mint this ledger tracks fees for.

fields.name: creator_wallet
docs: Phase 6.2 forward-compat: `creator_wallet` MUST stay mutable here so
a future `update_creator_wallet` instruction can swap it without a
program redeploy. Do NOT add an `#[account(constraint = ...)]` that
freezes this field; do NOT mark it `#[account(init_if_needed)]`-only.
Per D-03 (CONTEXT.md). See Open Question #4 (RESOLVED — deferred to
Phase 6.2 design for timelock semantics).

fields.name: accrued
docs: Total Meteora trading fees accrued (lamports). Monotonically increasing.
Credited by `claim_meteora_fees` (sweep-side).

fields.name: claimed
docs: Total claimed by creator (lamports). Monotonically increasing.
Incremented by `claim_creator_reward` (user-signed).

fields.name: last_swept_at
docs: Last time a sweep deposited fees (i64 unix seconds; 0 = never).

fields.name: last_claim_at
docs: Last time creator claimed (i64 unix seconds; 0 = never).

fields.name: reserved
docs: Reserved for v2 features (multi-recipient, vesting, expiry, per-room
claim_fee_bps override). Pad to 64 bytes so v2 migrations can add
fields without requiring a redeploy.

types.name: FeesClaimed
docs: Emitted by `claim_creator_reward` after a successful user-signed claim.
Memeputer has a single claim type (creator), so we don't carry a
`claim_type` enum like hey_curve does.

fields.name: mint
docs: The token mint whose FeeLedger was debited.

fields.name: claimer
docs: The signer (must == FeeLedger.creator_wallet by constraint).

fields.name: recipient
docs: The wallet that received the NET proceeds (defaults to creator, but
per D-07 the user may direct the net to any address).

fields.name: gross
docs: Lamports of the gross claim (= accrued - claimed BEFORE this call).

fields.name: claim_fee
docs: Lamports deducted as platform claim_fee (gross * claim_fee_bps / 10000).

fields.name: net
docs: Lamports paid to `recipient` (= gross - claim_fee).

fields.name: timestamp
docs: Clock::get() timestamp at emit time.

types.name: MeteoraFeesClaimed
docs: Emitted by `claim_meteora_fees` after a successful sweep deposit.
Memeputer is SOL-quoted only (DBC quote = wSOL → unwrapped to SOL on
deposit), so we don't carry a `token_claimed` field like hey_curve does.

fields.name: mint
docs: The token mint whose FeeLedger was credited.

fields.name: meteora_pool
docs: The Meteora pool the fees came from (DBC pre-graduation, or DAMM v2
position post-graduation).

fields.name: sol_claimed
docs: Lamports deposited into FeeVault by this sweep.

fields.name: timestamp
docs: Clock::get() timestamp at emit time.

types.name: PlatformConfig
docs: Singleton platform config (seeds: [b"platform_config"]).
Ported (with reductions) from `hey_curve/state/platform_config.rs`:
- KEEP: `bump`, `authority` (= upgrade authority), `claim_fee_bps`,
`platform_fee_recipient`.
- DROP: every bonding-curve field (`bonding_threshold_sol`,
`max_buy_sol`, `max_holding_bps`, `creator_sell_lock_seconds`,
`creation_fee_lamports`, `creator_fee_bps`, `migration_fee_lamports`,
`max_creator_fee_bps`). Memeputer uses Meteora DBC as the curve, so
these are configured at coin-launch time via the Meteora SDK, not
here.
- DROP: three-tier authority fields (`admin`, `operator`, `frozen`).
v1 uses a single `authority` field. Multi-sig + freeze flag deferred.
- ADD: `reserved: [u8; 64]` for v2 forward-compat.

fields.name: bump
docs: Canonical PDA bump (SEC-01).

fields.name: authority
docs: Upgrade authority — the only key that can call
`update_platform_config` or `transfer_upgrade_authority`. Note that
this is the program-level config authority, NOT the BPF loader's
program-upgrade authority (which is set via `solana program deploy
--keypair $UPGRADE_AUTHORITY_KEYPAIR` at deploy time).

fields.name: claim_fee_bps
docs: Claim fee in basis points deducted from `claim_creator_reward`
proceeds (default 100 = 1%; capped at 1000 = 10%). Per D-06: GLOBAL,
not per-room.

fields.name: platform_fee_recipient
docs: Single platform fee recipient wallet — receives the `claim_fee`
deduction from every `claim_creator_reward` call.

fields.name: reserved
docs: Reserved for v2 features (e.g., per-room claim_fee_bps override,
multi-recipient platform fees). Pad to 64 bytes.
